    "Title: Prometheus + Grafana Monitoring\nDescription: Monitoring configuration with Prometheus scrape config, alerting rules, and Grafana dashboard\nWhen to use: Production monitoring stack\nCode Example:\n# prometheus.yml\nscrape_configs:\n- job_name: spring-boot\n  metrics_path: /actuator/prometheus\n  static_configs: [{targets: ['api:8080']}]\n\n# alert.rules.yml\ngroups:\n- name: api\n  rules:\n  - alert: HighErrorRate\n    expr: rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{status=~\"5..\"}[5m]) / rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count[5m]) > 0.05\n    for: 5m\n    labels: {severity: critical}\n  - alert: HighLatency\n    expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket[5m])) > 2\n    for: 5m\n"
  ],
  "978d831c6a85644d5de992a12dd237cd": [
    "Full Stack Docker Compose",
    "Title: Full Stack Docker Compose\nDescription: Complete Docker Compose with API, PostgreSQL, and Redis for local development\n"
  ],
  "af56d36073adbb871d1de7a8da52b185": [
    "GitHub Actions Cloud Run Deploy",
    "Title: GitHub Actions Cloud Run Deploy\nDescription: GitHub Actions workflow for building and deploying to Google Cloud Run\n"
  ],
  "8619ca8a8320af15a6f0586dec4506a2": [
    "Nginx Reverse Proxy",
    "Title: Nginx Reverse Proxy\nDescription: Nginx config with HTTPS redirect, SSL, reverse proxy to backend, and SPA routing\n"
  ],
  "a1acf9d5b361f0352034da2e6116e706": [
    "Terraform Cloud Run",
    "Title: Terraform Cloud Run\nDescription: Terraform config for Google Cloud Run with secrets and auto-scaling\n"
  ],
  "08055023a679e67749e7fc9b00a65389": [
    "java.lang.NullPointerException",
    "Title: java.lang.NullPointerException\nCause: Accessing method or field on null reference\nSolution/Fix: Use Optional<T> for nullable returns. Add null checks. Enable @NonNull annotations. Use Objects.requireNonNull() at boundaries.\n"
  ],
  "41444a98ce607a9c0356562c36ce5ebc": [
    "java.lang.ClassNotFoundException / NoClassDefFoundError",
    "Title: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException / NoClassDefFoundError\nCause: Missing dependency or wrong classpath configuration\nSolution/Fix: Check build.gradle/pom.xml for missing dependency. Run ./gradlew dependencies to check resolution. Clean and rebuild: ./gradlew clean build.\n"
  ],
  "37ff73750c3379463c47df5e111bb27e": [
    "NoSuchBeanDefinitionException: No qualifying bean of type",
    "Title: NoSuchBeanDefinitionException: No qualifying bean of type\nCause: Missing @Component/@Service/@Repository annotation or component scan not covering package\nSolution/Fix: Add @Service/@Component to the class. Check @SpringBootApplication is in parent package. Add @ComponentScan if needed. Check @Configuration imports.\n"
  ],
  "d089557f90a47127ed0845477fbb0f51": [
    "BeanCurrentlyInCreationException: Requested bean is currently in creation: circular reference",
    "Title: BeanCurrentlyInCreationException: Requested bean is currently in creation: circular reference\nCause: Two or more beans depend on each other creating a cycle\nSolution/Fix: Use @Lazy on one dependency. Refactor to break cycle with events or a mediator. Use setter injection instead of constructor injection for one side.\n"
  ],
  "2fe9c39320a730d69129b954f896f363": [
    "Whitelabel Error Page - 404 Not Found for REST endpoint",
    "Title: Whitelabel Error Page - 404 Not Found for REST endpoint\nCause: Controller not scanned, wrong @RequestMapping path, or missing @RestController\nSolution/Fix: Verify @RestController (not @Controller for REST). Check @RequestMapping prefix matches URL. Ensure controller package is under @SpringBootApplication package.\n"
  ],
  "e5b1151b579b622b4169a94875238aa0": [
    "LazyInitializationException: could not initialize proxy - no Session",
    "Title: LazyInitializationException: could not initialize proxy - no Session\nCause: Accessing lazy-loaded relation outside Hibernate session/transaction\nSolution/Fix: Use JOIN FETCH in JPQL query. Use @EntityGraph on repository method. Use DTO projection instead of entity. Set spring.jpa.open-in-view=false and fetch eagerly where needed.\n"
  ],
  "28368e956122094870c868c6a4974ac3": [
    "PersistentObjectException: detached entity passed to persist",
    "Title: PersistentObjectException: detached entity passed to persist\nCause: Trying to persist an entity that already has an ID or was previously managed\nSolution/Fix: Use merge() instead of persist() for existing entities. Use save() which handles both. Remove manual ID setting if using @GeneratedValue.\n"
  ],
  "3c07d2ebf689824183829b72431eea63": [
    "CORS: Response to preflight request doesn't pass access control check",
    "Title: CORS: Response to preflight request doesn't pass access control check\nCause: Missing or incorrect CORS configuration in Spring Security\nSolution/Fix: Add CORS config in SecurityFilterChain: http.cors(c -> c.configurationSource(corsConfig())). Define CorsConfiguration with allowed origins, methods, headers. Ensure OPTIONS is permitted.\n"
  ],
  "3a82244c6d347f3428e12a42ca8463db": [
    "Could not resolve all files for configuration ':classpath'. Could not resolve com.android.tools.buil",
    "Title: Could not resolve all files for configuration ':classpath'. Could not resolve com.android.tools.build:gradle\nCause: Gradle version incompatible with plugin or JDK version\nSolution/Fix: Update gradle wrapper: ./gradlew wrapper --gradle-version=8.5. Check JDK compatibility. Update plugin version in build.gradle.kts. Check distributionUrl in gradle-wrapper.properties.\n"
  ],
  "c50f7cef3c5cdbe3573713ae923ec45a": [
    "java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space",
    "Title: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space\nCause: JVM heap exceeded due to large data processing, memory leaks, or insufficient allocation\nSolution/Fix: Increase heap: -Xmx512m. Check for memory leaks with profiler (VisualVM, JFR). Use streaming instead of loading all data. Check for unclosed resources (connections, streams).\n"
  ],
  "63fe8161e0c2101dfc3116294d8599d8": [
    "ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'xxx'",
    "Title: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'xxx'\nCause: Package not installed or wrong virtual environment\nSolution/Fix: Install: pip install xxx. Check venv is activated: which python. Add to requirements.txt. Check for typos in import. Verify __init__.py exists in package directories.\n"
  ],
  "dd09c2981a2a309223192ef2e3ba6fad": [
    "IndentationError: unexpected indent / unindent does not match",
    "Title: IndentationError: unexpected indent / unindent does not match\nCause: Mixing tabs and spaces or inconsistent indentation\nSolution/Fix: Use spaces only (4 spaces per level). Configure editor: insert spaces, tab size 4. Run: python -tt script.py to detect. autopep8 or black for auto-formatting.\n"
  ],
  "104835b4240f41c9d8070e04a7559a60": [
    "TypeError: can only concatenate str (not \"int\") to str",
    "Title: TypeError: can only concatenate str (not \"int\") to str\nCause: Mixing types in operations without conversion\nSolution/Fix: Use f-strings: f'Count: {count}'. Use str() for explicit conversion. Use type hints to catch at dev time. Enable mypy strict mode.\n"
  ],
  "24db6b668882f4df78d159bc690ff582": [
    "RuntimeError: This event loop is already running / coroutine was never awaited",
    "Title: RuntimeError: This event loop is already running / coroutine was never awaited\nCause: Missing await keyword or nested event loops\nSolution/Fix: Add 'await' before async function calls. Don't use asyncio.run() inside an already running loop. Use nest_asyncio if needed in Jupyter. Check async def uses await for all async calls.\n"
  ],
  "c1877f5fb6f2677feeb21662b1c9c1bd": [
    "422 Unprocessable Entity on FastAPI endpoint",
    "Title: 422 Unprocessable Entity on FastAPI endpoint\nCause: Request body doesn't match Pydantic model validation\nSolution/Fix: Check request Content-Type is application/json. Verify request body matches Pydantic model fields exactly. Check field types and constraints. Add validation error handler for readable messages.\n"
  ],
  "e28c7a9a6e104f66197f9614ae1235ea": [
    "TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'xxx')",
    "Title: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'xxx')\nCause: Accessing property on undefined variable, often from async data or missing null check\nSolution/Fix: Use optional chaining: obj?.property. Check data is loaded before accessing. Initialize state with default values. Use TypeScript strict mode for compile-time null checks.\n"
  ],
  "f7e4f0e4c3fd8d078b0a5ca913d57528": [
    "TypeError: Failed to fetch / CORS error in browser console",
    "Title: TypeError: Failed to fetch / CORS error in browser console\nCause: Backend doesn't return proper CORS headers or URL is wrong\nSolution/Fix: Configure CORS on backend (allow origin, methods, headers). Check URL is correct (http vs https, port). Use proxy in development (Vite proxy, CRA proxy). Check network tab for actual error.\n"
  ],
  "b32b37722aaf300681cbbb0c93118468": [
    "Type 'X' is not assignable to type 'Y'",
    "Title: Type 'X' is not assignable to type 'Y'\nCause: TypeScript type incompatibility between assignment and declaration\nSolution/Fix: Check types match. Use type assertion with 'as' only when certain. Use utility types: Partial<T>, Pick<T>, Omit<T>. Add missing fields or update type definition.\n"
  ],
  "98390328f53f8000d25ae010d5abbee9": [
    "Error: Invalid hook call. Hooks can only be called inside the body of a function component",
    "Title: Error: Invalid hook call. Hooks can only be called inside the body of a function component\nCause: Hook called outside component, in conditional, or duplicate React versions\nSolution/Fix: Only call hooks at top level of function components. Don't call in conditions/loops. Check for duplicate React in node_modules: npm ls react. Ensure component name starts with uppercase.\n"
  ],
  "4e21665a0379116c748c0a0c7f60fb72": [
    "Warning: Each child in a list should have a unique 'key' prop",
    "Title: Warning: Each child in a list should have a unique 'key' prop\nCause: Missing or non-unique key prop in list rendering\nSolution/Fix: Add unique key prop: items.map(item => <Item key={item.id} />). Use stable IDs, never array index for dynamic lists. Key must be unique among siblings.\n"
  ],
  "8491cf68d3e87814ae71c3919ed427c3": [
    "Error: Too many re-renders. React limits the number of renders to prevent an infinite loop",
    "Title: Error: Too many re-renders. React limits the number of renders to prevent an infinite loop\nCause: setState called during render or in useEffect without proper dependency array\nSolution/Fix: Don't call setState directly in component body. Use useEffect with proper deps array. Use callback form: onClick={() => setCount(c => c+1)} not onClick={setCount(count+1)}.\n"
  ],
  "8c43f5dbd123c5ca6528fdd02604a98e": [
    "Error: Hydration failed because the initial UI does not match what was rendered on the server",
    "Title: Error: Hydration failed because the initial UI does not match what was rendered on the server\nCause: Server and client render different HTML, often due to browser-only APIs or date/time\nSolution/Fix: Use useEffect for browser-only code. Suppress with suppressHydrationWarning for intentional differences. Use dynamic import with ssr:false for client-only components.\n"
  ],
  "c1fafa541c36af553fd7f073b30a1647": [
    "npm ERR! Could not resolve dependency: peer dep conflict",
    "Title: npm ERR! Could not resolve dependency: peer dep conflict\nCause: Conflicting peer dependency versions between packages\nSolution/Fix: Try: npm install --legacy-peer-deps. Or: update conflicting packages. Check which package requires the peer dep: npm explain <package>. Use overrides in package.json if needed.\n"
  ],
  "2cc63772bf7c777dc671a126b62f30ea": [
    "ERR_REQUIRE_ESM: Must use import to load ES Module / Cannot use import statement outside a module",
    "Title: ERR_REQUIRE_ESM: Must use import to load ES Module / Cannot use import statement outside a module\nCause: Mixing CommonJS and ES Module syntax\nSolution/Fix: Set 'type': 'module' in package.json for ESM. Use .mjs extension for ESM files. Use .cjs for CommonJS. Use dynamic import() for importing ESM in CJS.\n"
  ],
  "cc195c910ba3be7aa83a542f1ace2f1a": [
    "Null check operator used on a null value",
    "Title: Null check operator used on a null value\nCause: Using ! operator on a null value at runtime\nSolution/Fix: Replace ! with proper null check: if (value != null). Use ?? for default values. Use ?. for optional chaining. Check API response can be null.\n"
  ],
  "cc83734f89d5ba3fa7de57980430285a": [
    "A RenderFlex overflowed by N pixels on the right/bottom",
    "Title: A RenderFlex overflowed by N pixels on the right/bottom\nCause: Widget exceeds parent container bounds\nSolution/Fix: Wrap in SingleChildScrollView for scrolling. Use Flexible/Expanded in Row/Column. Set overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis for text. Use ConstrainedBox or SizedBox for explicit bounds.\n"
  ],
  "6e37ca89dcd24a9bf752ac613b63a874": [
    "setState() called after dispose()",
    "Title: setState() called after dispose()\nCause: Async operation completes after widget is removed from tree\nSolution/Fix: Check mounted before setState: if(mounted) setState(() {}). Cancel subscriptions in dispose(). Use CancelableOperation. With Riverpod: ref.onDispose() to cancel.\n"
  ],
  "a5992c7a3e5204a7ed47209f4770d6b2": [
    "Execution failed for task ':app:compileDebugKotlin' / Flutter build failed",
    "Title: Execution failed for task ':app:compileDebugKotlin' / Flutter build failed\nCause: Gradle/Kotlin version mismatch or corrupted build cache\nSolution/Fix: Clean build: flutter clean && flutter pub get && cd android && ./gradlew clean && cd .. && flutter build. Update Kotlin version in android/build.gradle. Check minSdkVersion.\n"
  ],
  "d51176df8fe3fd72f98d11a5b7aec01c": [
    "CocoaPods could not find compatible versions for pod / CDN: trunk URL couldn't be downloaded",
    "Title: CocoaPods could not find compatible versions for pod / CDN: trunk URL couldn't be downloaded\nCause: iOS dependencies conflict or CocoaPods cache issue\nSolution/Fix: Clean: cd ios && pod deintegrate && pod cache clean --all && pod install. Update: pod repo update. Delete Podfile.lock and reinstall. Check minimum iOS version.\n"
  ],
  "ab714be77501d581813b3f07c0b535e6": [
    "Bind for 0.0.0.0:8080 failed: port is already allocated",
    "Title: Bind for 0.0.0.0:8080 failed: port is already allocated\nCause: Another process is using the port\nSolution/Fix: Find process: lsof -i :8080 (Linux/Mac) or netstat -ano | findstr :8080 (Windows). Kill process or use different port: -p 8081:8080.\n"
  ],
  "3eb2accb2fdacc80d2fd4e07acbc0bd1": [
    "Docker build not picking up changes / using cached layer",
    "Title: Docker build not picking up changes / using cached layer\nCause: Docker layer caching serving stale layers\nSolution/Fix: Use --no-cache for full rebuild. Restructure Dockerfile: COPY requirements first, then source code. Use .dockerignore to exclude node_modules, .git.\n"
  ],
  "6210631e6801e83eb1ca32178a328b0d": [
    "Container killed: OOMKilled / Exited (137)",
    "Title: Container killed: OOMKilled / Exited (137)\nCause: Container exceeded memory limit\nSolution/Fix: Increase memory limit: docker run -m 512m. For JVM: set -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0. Check for memory leaks. Use multi-stage build to reduce image size.\n"
  ],
  "5f25825dc8c2031253cc858004f0d7bf": [
    "Could not resolve host / Connection refused between containers",
    "Title: Could not resolve host / Connection refused between containers\nCause: Containers not on same Docker network or using wrong hostname\nSolution/Fix: Use service name as hostname in Docker Compose (not 127.0.0.1). Ensure services are on same network. Check container is actually running: docker ps. Use depends_on with healthcheck.\n"
  ],
  "1f9ec88a5960c97227d178d1ca2893d7": [
    "Pod in CrashLoopBackOff state",
    "Title: Pod in CrashLoopBackOff state\nCause: Container crashes on startup repeatedly\nSolution/Fix: Check logs: kubectl logs <pod> --previous. Check: wrong command, missing env vars, health check failing too fast. Increase initialDelaySeconds for liveness probe. Check resource limits.\n"
  ],
  "49375778804d5c5592c151aa4679f6e5": [
    "Pod in ImagePullBackOff state",
    "Title: Pod in ImagePullBackOff state\nCause: Cannot pull container image from registry\nSolution/Fix: Check image name and tag exist. Verify registry credentials: kubectl create secret docker-registry. Check network access to registry. Use kubectl describe pod for details.\n"
  ],
  "b01e1179cf957affc33f6c2352c58e5b": [
    "Pod stuck in Pending state",
    "Title: Pod stuck in Pending state\nCause: Insufficient cluster resources or scheduling constraints\nSolution/Fix: Check: kubectl describe pod <name> for events. Verify node resources: kubectl describe node. Check PVC is bound. Check nodeSelector/tolerations match available nodes.\n"
  ],
  "2a33a80b32527584c0409748d46b68b9": [
    "CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in <file>",
    "Title: CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in <file>\nCause: Both branches modified the same lines\nSolution/Fix: Open file, look for <<<<<<< markers. Choose correct version or combine. Stage resolved files: git add <file>. Continue: git merge --continue or git rebase --continue.\n"
  ],
  "36b2c4e90856a7becea9ed4fec1d6337": [
    "error: failed to push some refs to origin. Updates were rejected because the remote contains work",
    "Title: error: failed to push some refs to origin. Updates were rejected because the remote contains work\nCause: Remote has commits not in local branch\nSolution/Fix: Pull first: git pull --rebase origin main. Resolve any conflicts. Then push. NEVER force push to shared branches unless you know what you're doing.\n"
  ],
  "5469c49ce19c591fcf95a441b4f78c7a": [
    "You are in 'detached HEAD' state",
    "Title: You are in 'detached HEAD' state\nCause: Checked out a specific commit or tag instead of a branch\nSolution/Fix: Create branch from current state: git checkout -b new-branch. Or go back: git checkout main. Commits in detached HEAD are lost unless you create a branch.\n"
  ],
  "80c8c8c68a1fd71775de9122ac08a89e": [
    "remote: error: File xxx is 100 MB+ and exceeds GitHub's file size limit",
    "Title: remote: error: File xxx is 100 MB+ and exceeds GitHub's file size limit\nCause: Trying to push a file larger than GitHub's 100MB limit\nSolution/Fix: Add to .gitignore. Remove from history: git filter-branch or BFG Repo Cleaner. Use Git LFS for large files: git lfs track '*.zip'.\n"
  ],
  "c82f624a419b81a79307813fd74d483c": [
    "Gradle build daemon disappeared unexpectedly / Could not connect to Gradle daemon",
    "Title: Gradle build daemon disappeared unexpectedly / Could not connect to Gradle daemon\nCause: Gradle daemon crashed, usually due to memory or JDK issues\nSolution/Fix: Stop daemons: ./gradlew --stop. Increase memory: org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx2g in gradle.properties. Check JDK version compatibility. Delete .gradle cache.\n"
  ],
  "c0d1e5fd2e1017b6354262f23ffff6df": [
    "Module not found: Can't resolve './Component' in '/src'",
    "Title: Module not found: Can't resolve './Component' in '/src'\nCause: Missing file, wrong path, or missing file extension in import\nSolution/Fix: Check file exists at the exact path. Check case sensitivity (Linux is case-sensitive). Add extensions to resolve: resolve.extensions in webpack config. Check tsconfig paths.\n"
  ],
  "04f1c69f0618ea8308eff080afa28bec": [
    "[vite] hot module replacement (HMR) disconnected / WebSocket connection failed",
    "Title: [vite] hot module replacement (HMR) disconnected / WebSocket connection failed\nCause: Vite dev server WebSocket can't connect, often proxy or network issue\nSolution/Fix: Check server.hmr config in vite.config. Set server.hmr.host explicitly. If behind proxy: configure WebSocket proxy. Check firewall/antivirus not blocking.\n"
  ],
  "3c1945fcbef5575b33845dfe51d4bff9": [
    "FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions",
    "Title: FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions\nCause: Firestore/RTDB security rules blocking the operation\nSolution/Fix: Check security rules match the operation (read/write/create/update/delete). Verify user is authenticated. Check rule conditions. Test in Firebase emulator with debug logging.\n"
  ],
  "b5bf1e1e6ef16979b35e28226c664ba2": [
    "FirebaseError: The query requires an index. Create it at: https://...",
    "Title: FirebaseError: The query requires an index. Create it at: https://...\nCause: Composite index needed for the Firestore query\nSolution/Fix: Click the link in the error to auto-create the index. Or add to firestore.indexes.json. Index creation takes a few minutes. Add indexes proactively for common query patterns.\n"
  ],
  "20eb716b9fcb5239cce9ebc5d4dc0e00": [
    "FirebaseError: auth/user-not-found or auth/wrong-password",
    "Title: FirebaseError: auth/user-not-found or auth/wrong-password\nCause: User doesn't exist or password is incorrect\nSolution/Fix: Show generic error to user: 'Invalid email or password' (don't reveal which is wrong). Check email format. Implement rate limiting on auth endpoints. Check Auth provider is enabled.\n"
  ],
  "2fed93fd523319260a44eb3b58a7cf4d": [
    "Cloud Run: Container failed to start. Failed to start and then listen on the port defined by the POR",
    "Title: Cloud Run: Container failed to start. Failed to start and then listen on the port defined by the PORT environment variable\nCause: Container not listening on $PORT or taking too long to start\nSolution/Fix: Read PORT from env: int port = Integer.parseInt(System.getenv().getOrDefault(\"PORT\", \"8080\")). Reduce startup time. Increase startup timeout in Cloud Run settings. Check for missing env vars.\n"
  ],
  "6031291d02bf951684433a4e6580a455": [
    "403 Forbidden: The caller does not have permission / IAM permission denied",
    "Title: 403 Forbidden: The caller does not have permission / IAM permission denied\nCause: Service account missing required IAM role\nSolution/Fix: Check required roles in GCP docs. Add role: gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding PROJECT --member=serviceAccount:SA --role=roles/xxx. Check correct service account is being used.\n"
  ],
  "4c6fca3b5b273872e3d5d0494bcdc366": [
    "SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED / unable to verify the first certificate",
    "Title: SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED / unable to verify the first certificate\nCause: SSL certificate issue: expired, self-signed, or missing intermediate cert\nSolution/Fix: Check cert expiry: openssl s_client -connect host:443. Renew if expired. Add intermediate certs to chain. For dev only: disable verify (NEVER in production).\n"
  ],
  "f3917a4c21da1fb4f737f04df253098d": [
    "fw_spring_boot_structure",
    "Title: fw_spring_boot_structure\nContent: Spring Boot project structure: src/main/java/{pkg}/{controller,service,model,repository,config,dto,exception}. Application.java at root. application.yml for config. Use @SpringBootApplication on main class. Component scanning auto-detects @Service, @Controller, @Repository, @Component.\nSolutions: Controller: @RestController @RequestMapping(\"/api/v1/users\"), Service: @Service + @Transactional for business logic, Repository: extends JpaRepository<Entity, ID> for data access, Config: @Configuration + @Bean for third-party beans, DTO: Java records for request/response payloads\n"
  ],
  "e41527c9059759ceae7a5a983494f1ba": [
    "fw_spring_security",
    "Title: fw_spring_security\nContent: Spring Security 6+: SecurityFilterChain bean replaces WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter. JWT auth: OncePerRequestFilter extracts token → validates → sets SecurityContext. Use @PreAuthorize for method-level security. CORS config in SecurityFilterChain.\nSolutions: Config: @Bean SecurityFilterChain filterChain(HttpSecurity http) { return http.csrf(c->c.disable()).sessionManagement(s->s.sessionCreationPolicy(STATELESS)).authorizeHttpRequests(a->a.requestMatchers(\"/api/auth/**\").permitAll().anyRequest().authenticated()).addFilterBefore(jwtFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class).build(); }, JWT Filter: extract token from Authorization header → validate → UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken, Method security: @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('ADMIN')\") on controller methods, CORS: .cors(c -> c.configurationSource(corsConfig()))\n"
  ],
  "c79db1e9c0341f1ab21c3559dc78330e": [
    "fw_spring_data_jpa",
    "Title: fw_spring_data_jpa\nContent: Spring Data JPA: Define interface extending JpaRepository. Method name queries: findByEmailAndActive(String email, boolean active). @Query for complex queries. Projections for partial selects. Specification for dynamic queries. Pageable for pagination.\nSolutions: Query methods: findByStatusIn(List<Status> statuses), findByCreatedAtAfter(LocalDateTime date), Pagination: Page<User> findByActive(boolean active, Pageable pageable), Custom: @Query(\"SELECT new com.dto.UserSummary(u.name, u.email) FROM User u WHERE u.active = true\"), Specification: where(hasName(name)).and(isActive())\n"
  ],
  "bd843c1de66c12625f552e8d5b3e8dcd": [
    "fw_spring_validation",
    "Title: fw_spring_validation\nContent: Spring Validation: @Valid on @RequestBody. Use Jakarta validation annotations: @NotBlank, @Email, @Size, @Min, @Max, @Pattern. Custom validators with @Constraint. MethodArgumentNotValidException handled by @ControllerAdvice.\nSolutions: DTO: record CreateUser(@NotBlank String name, @Email String email, @Size(min=8) String password) {}, Controller: @PostMapping public User create(@Valid @RequestBody CreateUser dto), Custom: @Constraint(validatedBy=PhoneValidator.class) @interface ValidPhone {}, Error handler: @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class) returns field errors\n"
  ],
  "a300ae51629498949422bfa948d876e8": [
    "fw_spring_actuator",
    "Title: fw_spring_actuator\nContent: Spring Actuator: Health checks, metrics, info endpoints. Enable with spring-boot-starter-actuator. Expose specific endpoints: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics,prometheus. Custom health indicators with HealthIndicator interface. Prometheus integration for monitoring.\nSolutions: Config: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,prometheus, Custom: @Component class DbHealthIndicator implements HealthIndicator { Health health() { return db.isConnected() ? Health.up().build() : Health.down().build(); } }, Prometheus: add micrometer-registry-prometheus dependency, Info: management.info.env.enabled=true + info.app.name=SupremeAI in yml\n"
  ],
  "b7d69aac2f0c9b629225d0c1fbf0d0cb": [
    "fw_react_hooks",
    "Title: fw_react_hooks\nContent: React Hooks: useState for local state, useEffect for side effects (cleanup!), useCallback for stable callbacks, useMemo for expensive computations, useRef for DOM/values. Custom hooks extract reusable logic. Rules: only call at top level, only in components/hooks.\nSolutions: State: const [users, setUsers] = useState<User[]>([]), Effect: useEffect(() => { const ctrl = new AbortController(); fetchData(ctrl.signal); return () => ctrl.abort(); }, [id]), Callback: const handleClick = useCallback((id: string) => setSelected(id), []), Custom: function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T { ... }\n"
  ],
  "ac567fc6dcbaa6313de55e26766ad811": [
    "fw_react_state_management",
    "Title: fw_react_state_management\nContent: React state management tiers: (1) useState for component state, (2) useContext for theme/auth/locale, (3) Zustand/Jotai for client state, (4) TanStack Query for server state. Don't use Redux for new projects — Zustand is simpler. TanStack Query replaces most useEffect data fetching.\nSolutions: Server state: const { data, isLoading } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['users'], queryFn: fetchUsers }), Client state: const useStore = create<State>((set) => ({ count: 0, inc: () => set(s => ({ count: s.count + 1 })) })), Context: const ThemeContext = createContext<Theme>('light'); <ThemeContext.Provider value={theme}>, Mutation: const mutation = useMutation({ mutationFn: createUser, onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries(['users']) })\n"
  ],
  "24a9c8e50f6ccf9acff58f34f94132b6": [
    "fw_react_performance",
    "Title: fw_react_performance\nContent: React performance: React.memo for pure components. useMemo/useCallback to prevent re-renders. React.lazy + Suspense for code splitting. Use React DevTools Profiler. Virtualize long lists with react-window. Avoid inline objects/arrays in JSX props.\nSolutions: Memo: const UserCard = React.memo(({ user }: { user: User }) => <div>{user.name}</div>), Lazy: const Dashboard = React.lazy(() => import('./Dashboard')), Virtual: <FixedSizeList height={600} width={400} itemSize={50} itemCount={items.length}>{Row}</FixedSizeList>, Key: Always use stable IDs as keys, never array index for dynamic lists\n"
  ],
  "9cd0bf1a4dcf5025054918b1ace6b767": [
    "fw_react_forms",
    "Title: fw_react_forms\nContent: React forms: Use react-hook-form for performance (uncontrolled inputs). Zod for schema validation with zodResolver. Use FormProvider for nested forms. Controlled inputs only when you need real-time validation display.\nSolutions: Setup: const { register, handleSubmit, formState: { errors } } = useForm<FormData>({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) }), Schema: const schema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), password: z.string().min(8) }), Submit: <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)}><input {...register('email')} />, Error: {errors.email && <span>{errors.email.message}</span>}\n"
  ],
  "74796578df4a034dbde9e7be2e57b15a": [
    "fw_flutter_architecture",
    "Title: fw_flutter_architecture\nContent: Flutter clean architecture: lib/{core,features,shared}. Each feature: {data,domain,presentation}. Data: repositories, data sources, models. Domain: entities, use cases, repository interfaces. Presentation: pages, widgets, state management (BLoC/Riverpod).\nSolutions: Structure: lib/features/auth/{data/{repos,sources,models}, domain/{entities,usecases,repos}, presentation/{pages,widgets,bloc}}, Use case: class GetUser { final UserRepository repo; Future<User> call(String id) => repo.getUser(id); }, Repository: abstract class UserRepository { Future<User> getUser(String id); }, Dependency injection: Use get_it or riverpod for DI\n"
  ],
  "aeceb04eb356514091df62b74756225b": [
    "fw_flutter_navigation",
    "Title: fw_flutter_navigation\nContent: Flutter navigation: Use go_router for declarative routing. Define routes with GoRouter. ShellRoute for persistent navigation (bottom nav bar). Redirect for auth guards. Use path parameters and query parameters for deep linking.\nSolutions: Router: final router = GoRouter(routes: [GoRoute(path: '/', builder: (ctx, state) => HomePage()), GoRoute(path: '/user/:id', builder: (ctx, state) => UserPage(id: state.pathParameters['id']!))]), Shell: ShellRoute(builder: (ctx, state, child) => ScaffoldWithNav(child: child), routes: [...]), Redirect: redirect: (ctx, state) { if (!isLoggedIn) return '/login'; return null; }, Navigate: context.go('/user/123'); context.push('/settings');\n"
  ],
  "2790ae30485b06104b569ab9fac1b6ab": [
    "fw_flutter_widgets",
    "Title: fw_flutter_widgets\nContent: Flutter widget best practices: Prefer const constructors. Extract widgets into separate classes over helper methods. Use Builder pattern for context access. Keys for lists and animations. CustomPainter for complex drawings. Slivers for advanced scrolling.\nSolutions: Const: class MyWidget extends StatelessWidget { const MyWidget({super.key}); }, Extract: class UserAvatar extends StatelessWidget {} // not Widget _buildAvatar(), ListView: ListView.builder(itemCount: items.length, itemBuilder: (ctx, i) => ListTile(key: ValueKey(items[i].id), title: Text(items[i].name))), Sliver: CustomScrollView(slivers: [SliverAppBar(...), SliverList(...)])\n"
  ],
  "8abf7082ab57df41231a4e7135d43a52": [
    "fw_express_middleware",
    "Title: fw_express_middleware\nContent: Express middleware chain: app.use(cors()), app.use(helmet()), app.use(express.json()), app.use(rateLimit()), routes, app.use(errorHandler). Order matters. Custom middleware: (req, res, next) => { /* logic */ next(); }. Error middleware has 4 params: (err, req, res, next).\nSolutions: Auth: const auth = (req, res, next) => { const token = req.headers.authorization?.split(' ')[1]; if(!token) return res.status(401).json({error:'No token'}); try { req.user = jwt.verify(token, SECRET); next(); } catch { res.status(403).json({error:'Invalid token'}); } }, Error: app.use((err, req, res, next) => { console.error(err); res.status(err.status||500).json({error:err.message}); }), Rate limit: app.use(rateLimit({windowMs:15*60*1000, max:100})), Security: app.use(helmet()); app.use(cors({origin:ALLOWED_ORIGINS}));\n"
  ],
  "0c75486c9796f7f8d3b4147775f36283": [
    "fw_express_structure",
    "Title: fw_express_structure\nContent: Express project structure: src/{routes,controllers,services,models,middleware,utils,config}. Route files define endpoints. Controllers handle request/response. Services contain business logic. Models define data shapes. Keep routes thin — delegate to controllers → services.\nSolutions: Route: router.get('/', userController.list); router.post('/', validate(createSchema), userController.create);, Controller: const list = async (req, res, next) => { try { const users = await userService.findAll(req.query); res.json(users); } catch(e) { next(e); } }, Service: class UserService { async findAll(filters) { return UserModel.find(filters).lean(); } }, Index: app.use('/api/v1/users', authMiddleware, userRoutes);\n"
  ],
  "f42395c64f69a3dc340afcf71af2076a": [
    "fw_fastapi_structure",
    "Title: fw_fastapi_structure\nContent: FastAPI project structure: app/{main.py,routers/,models/,schemas/,services/,core/,db/}. Use APIRouter for modular routes. Pydantic BaseModel for request/response schemas. Depends() for dependency injection. BackgroundTasks for async jobs. Lifespan for startup/shutdown.\nSolutions: Router: router = APIRouter(prefix='/users', tags=['users']); @router.get('/', response_model=list[UserOut]), Schema: class UserCreate(BaseModel): name: str = Field(min_length=1); email: EmailStr, Depends: async def get_db(): async with SessionLocal() as session: yield session, Lifespan: @asynccontextmanager async def lifespan(app): await init_db(); yield; await close_db()\n"
  ],
  "c8e449e9b60af535317b4bd445e2d378": [
    "fw_fastapi_auth",
    "Title: fw_fastapi_auth\nContent: FastAPI auth: OAuth2PasswordBearer for JWT flow. Dependency injection for current user. HTTPBearer for API keys. Security scopes for role-based access. Password hashing with passlib/bcrypt.\nSolutions: Token: oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl='token'), Dependency: async def get_current_user(token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme)): payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET); return await get_user(payload['sub']), Protected: @router.get('/me') async def me(user: User = Depends(get_current_user)): return user, Hash: pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=['bcrypt']); hashed = pwd_context.hash(password)\n"
  ],
  "c432fa847c760ccd88ca0eb48125507a": [
    "fw_nextjs_app_router",
    "Title: fw_nextjs_app_router\nContent: Next.js App Router (13+): app/ directory with page.tsx, layout.tsx, loading.tsx, error.tsx. Server Components by default — add 'use client' for client interactivity. Server Actions for mutations. Parallel routes with @folder. Route groups with (folder).\nSolutions: Page: app/users/[id]/page.tsx exports default async function UserPage({ params }), Layout: app/layout.tsx — persistent layout wrapping all pages, Server Action: 'use server'; async function createUser(formData: FormData) { await db.insert(...); revalidatePath('/users'); }, Loading: app/users/loading.tsx — automatic Suspense boundary\n"
  ],
  "672025b72da485547a3b51b57513d91d": [
    "fw_nextjs_data_fetching",
    "Title: fw_nextjs_data_fetching\nContent: Next.js data fetching: Server Components fetch directly (no useEffect). Use fetch() with cache/revalidate options. generateStaticParams for SSG. unstable_cache for function-level caching. revalidatePath/revalidateTag for on-demand ISR.\nSolutions: Server fetch: const users = await fetch(API_URL, { next: { revalidate: 60 } }).then(r => r.json()), SSG: export async function generateStaticParams() { return posts.map(p => ({ slug: p.slug })); }, Revalidate: revalidateTag('users') after mutation, Client: Use TanStack Query for client-side data that needs frequent updates\n"
  ],
  "b8502bcd7b39abcd300295aa916df002": [
    "fw_django_rest_framework",
    "Title: fw_django_rest_framework\nContent: Django REST Framework: ModelSerializer for auto CRUD. ViewSet + Router for URL generation. Permission classes for access control. Pagination, filtering, throttling built-in. Use DRF serializers for validation — not Django forms in API views.\nSolutions: Serializer: class UserSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer): class Meta: model = User; fields = '__all__', ViewSet: class UserViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet): queryset = User.objects.all(); serializer_class = UserSerializer; permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated], Router: router = DefaultRouter(); router.register('users', UserViewSet), Filter: filter_backends = [DjangoFilterBackend, SearchFilter, OrderingFilter]\n"
  ],
  "ea3e141a3d94c28b43eed4ef140c0c1a": [
    "fw_nestjs_modules",
    "Title: fw_nestjs_modules\nContent: NestJS architecture: Modules encapsulate features. Each module has controllers, providers (services), imports. Use @Injectable() for services. @Controller() for routes. Pipes for validation. Guards for auth. Interceptors for transform/cache.\nSolutions: Module: @Module({ controllers: [UserController], providers: [UserService], imports: [TypeOrmModule.forFeature([User])] }) export class UserModule {}, Service: @Injectable() export class UserService { constructor(@InjectRepository(User) private repo: Repository<User>) {} }, Controller: @Controller('users') export class UserController { @Get() findAll() { return this.userService.findAll(); } }, Guard: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) @Get('profile') getProfile(@Req() req) { return req.user; }\n"
  ],
  "98c7d207708a2a3940bb319f419a2e13": [
    "Spring REST Controller",
    "Title: Spring REST Controller\nDescription: Complete REST controller with CRUD, validation, pagination, and error handling\nWhen to use: API layer in Spring Boot applications\nCode Example:\n@RestController @RequestMapping(\"/api/v1/users\") @RequiredArgsConstructor public class UserController { private final UserService service; @GetMapping public Page<UserDTO> list(Pageable pageable) { return service.findAll(pageable); } @GetMapping(\"/{id}\") public UserDTO get(@PathVariable Long id) { return service.findById(id); } @PostMapping @ResponseStatus(CREATED) public UserDTO create(@Valid @RequestBody CreateUserDTO dto) { return service.create(dto); } @PutMapping(\"/{id}\") public UserDTO update(@PathVariable Long id, @Valid @RequestBody UpdateUserDTO dto) { return service.update(id, dto); } @DeleteMapping(\"/{id}\") @ResponseStatus(NO_CONTENT) public void delete(@PathVariable Long id) { service.delete(id); } }\n"
  ],
  "9bd7f09bdd35f104503b4df5084c9eff": [
    "Global Exception Handler",
    "Title: Global Exception Handler\nDescription: ControllerAdvice for centralized error handling with problem details\nWhen to use: Error handling across all Spring Boot controllers\nCode Example:\n@ControllerAdvice public class GlobalExceptionHandler { @ExceptionHandler(EntityNotFoundException.class) public ProblemDetail handleNotFound(EntityNotFoundException e) { ProblemDetail pd = ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, e.getMessage()); pd.setTitle(\"Not Found\"); return pd; } @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class) public ProblemDetail handleValidation(MethodArgumentNotValidException e) { ProblemDetail pd = ProblemDetail.forStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST); pd.setTitle(\"Validation Failed\"); pd.setProperty(\"errors\", e.getFieldErrors().stream().map(f -> Map.of(\"field\",f.getField(),\"message\",f.getDefaultMessage())).toList()); return pd; } }\n"
  ],
  "7be5fdf0a50bb2b298ed49267ad92c13": [
    "React Page Component",
    "Title: React Page Component\nDescription: Full page component with data fetching, loading/error states, and TypeScript\nWhen to use: Page-level components in React SPAs\nCode Example:\nexport default function UsersPage() { const { data: users, isLoading, error } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['users'], queryFn: fetchUsers }); if(isLoading) return <Skeleton count={5} />; if(error) return <ErrorAlert message={error.message} />; return (<div className='container'><h1>Users</h1><DataTable columns={columns} data={users} /><CreateUserDialog /></div>); }\n"
  ],
  "01cd8b1af1b8aa5c49a795324dfe6d32": [
    "Flutter Screen Template",
    "Title: Flutter Screen Template\nDescription: Stateful screen with BLoC/Riverpod state, error handling, pull to refresh\nWhen to use: Feature screens in Flutter apps\nCode Example:\nclass UsersScreen extends ConsumerWidget { const UsersScreen({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) { final usersAsync = ref.watch(usersProvider); return Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('Users')), body: usersAsync.when(data: (users) => RefreshIndicator(onRefresh: () => ref.refresh(usersProvider.future), child: ListView.builder(itemCount: users.length, itemBuilder: (ctx, i) => UserTile(user: users[i]))), loading: () => const Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator()), error: (e, s) => ErrorWidget(message: e.toString(), onRetry: () => ref.invalidate(usersProvider)))); } }\n"
  ],
  "17c8fe9bb5dc100855cb55af48c4f1b9": [
    "Next.js Server Action",
    "Title: Next.js Server Action\nDescription: Server action with validation, database mutation, and revalidation\nWhen to use: Form submissions and mutations in Next.js App Router\nCode Example:\n'use server'; import { z } from 'zod'; import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'; const schema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), email: z.string().email() }); export async function createUser(formData: FormData) { const parsed = schema.safeParse(Object.fromEntries(formData)); if(!parsed.success) return { error: parsed.error.flatten() }; await db.user.create({ data: parsed.data }); revalidatePath('/users'); return { success: true }; }\n"
  ],
  "c99f19b597e34d43d1c906c78e1620e7": [
    "Spring Boot application.yml",
    "Title: Spring Boot application.yml\nDescription: Production-ready Spring Boot configuration with security, JPA, Jackson, and Actuator settings\n"
  ],
  "be9e7b1a7ac72c1dfc7a8af0c7126087": [
    "React App Layout",
    "Title: React App Layout\nDescription: Standard app layout with sidebar, header, and content area using React Router outlet\n"
  ],
  "df20914db7b4c9c46e4b744f469b7d08": [
    "Flutter main.dart",
    "Title: Flutter main.dart\nDescription: Flutter app entry point with Riverpod, theming, and go_router setup\n"
  ],
  "c375833412fd97ac7653c6caa8ebd7e0": [
    "Spring Boot Dockerfile",
    "Title: Spring Boot Dockerfile\nDescription: Multi-stage Dockerfile for Spring Boot with health check and JVM tuning\n"
  ],
  "683a357893607cab9d3b950d21cbc648": [
    "Express Server Setup",
    "Title: Express Server Setup\nDescription: Express server with security middleware, rate limiting, and structured routes\n"
  ],
  "f5c1f519e3e04ebc8e9a43bdf33c3ad6": [
    "FastAPI Main",
    "Title: FastAPI Main\nDescription: FastAPI application with lifespan, CORS, and modular routers\n"
  ],
  "70d96e045ccb33960d3b817b5f037ada": [
    "lang_java_streams",
    "Title: lang_java_streams\nContent: Java Streams API: Use .stream().filter().map().collect() for collection transformations. Parallel streams with .parallelStream() for CPU-bound tasks on large datasets (>10K elements). Avoid parallel streams for IO-bound work.\nSolutions: Use Collectors.toList(), toMap(), groupingBy() for terminal operations, Prefer method references (String::toLowerCase) over lambdas when possible, Use Optional.ofNullable() to avoid NPE in stream chains\n"
  ],
  "c9511c26e22d9194338a350871ded5b0": [
    "lang_java_records",
    "Title: lang_java_records\nContent: Java Records (16+): Use 'record ClassName(Type field)' for immutable data carriers. Auto-generates equals(), hashCode(), toString(), getters. Perfect for DTOs, API responses, value objects. Cannot extend classes but can implement interfaces.\nSolutions: Replace POJOs with records for DTOs: record UserDTO(String name, String email) {}, Use compact constructors for validation: record Age(int value) { Age { if(value<0) throw new IllegalArgumentException(); } }, Records can have static fields and methods but no instance fields beyond components\n"
  ],
  "831c66e51e3acaf4ca5d019c6a0dcbc2": [
    "lang_java_sealed_classes",
    "Title: lang_java_sealed_classes\nContent: Sealed classes (Java 17+): 'sealed class Shape permits Circle, Rectangle' restricts which classes can extend. Enables exhaustive pattern matching in switch. Use for domain models with finite subtypes.\nSolutions: Combine with records: sealed interface Shape permits Circle, Rectangle {}; record Circle(double r) implements Shape {}, Switch expressions with sealed types are exhaustive — no default needed, Use 'non-sealed' keyword to re-open hierarchy for specific subtypes\n"
  ],
  "ce640d7e7f00e173e9b473e8408bc750": [
    "lang_java_virtual_threads",
    "Title: lang_java_virtual_threads\nContent: Virtual Threads (Java 21+): Use Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor() for high-concurrency IO-bound tasks. 1M+ virtual threads possible. Don't use for CPU-bound work. Spring Boot 3.2+ supports virtual threads via spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true.\nSolutions: Enable in Spring Boot: spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true, Replace thread pools with virtual threads for HTTP clients, DB calls, Avoid synchronized blocks in virtual thread code — use ReentrantLock instead, Don't pool virtual threads — create new ones per task\n"
  ],
  "cf4858f9956f172618dc599cf147f30d": [
    "lang_java_optional",
    "Title: lang_java_optional\nContent: Optional usage: Return Optional<T> from methods that might not find a result. Never use Optional as method parameter or field. Use orElseThrow() for required values, orElse() for defaults, map()/flatMap() for transformations.\nSolutions: Service: return Optional.ofNullable(repository.findById(id)), Controller: .orElseThrow(() -> new ResponseStatusException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND)), Never: Optional<String> name as a parameter — use @Nullable or overloading instead, Chain: optional.map(User::getName).orElse(\"Anonymous\")\n"
  ],
  "27e5c52e2317d6a2a6c96775c70c0550": [
    "lang_java_generics",
    "Title: lang_java_generics\nContent: Generics best practices: Use bounded wildcards — '? extends T' for producers (read), '? super T' for consumers (write). PECS principle. Use type inference with diamond operator. Generic methods over generic classes when possible.\nSolutions: Producer: List<? extends Number> — can read Number, can't add, Consumer: List<? super Integer> — can add Integer, reads as Object, Generic method: <T extends Comparable<T>> T max(T a, T b), Type erasure means no 'new T()' or 'instanceof T' at runtime\n"
  ],
  "255c71379929109483e0606035cd62fd": [
    "lang_java_exceptions",
    "Title: lang_java_exceptions\nContent: Exception handling: Use unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException subclasses) for programming errors. Checked exceptions only for recoverable conditions the caller must handle. Create domain-specific exceptions. Use @ControllerAdvice for global handling in Spring.\nSolutions: Create: class OrderNotFoundException extends RuntimeException { OrderNotFoundException(Long id) { super(\"Order not found: \" + id); } }, Global handler: @ExceptionHandler(OrderNotFoundException.class) returns 404, Never catch Exception or Throwable — catch specific types, Use try-with-resources for ALL Closeable resources\n"
  ],
  "51994d415949ea90da1111730e2cc0dd": [
    "lang_python_typing",
    "Title: lang_python_typing\nContent: Python type hints: Use typing module for all function signatures. dict[str, Any], list[int], Optional[str], Union[str, int]. Use TypedDict for dict shapes. Pydantic BaseModel for runtime validation. mypy for static checking.\nSolutions: Function: def get_user(user_id: int) -> Optional[User]:, TypedDict: class UserDict(TypedDict): name: str; age: int, Pydantic: class UserCreate(BaseModel): name: str; email: EmailStr, Run mypy: mypy --strict src/\n"
  ],
  "62d0e1ee500606af041f2daf89686fcc": [
    "lang_python_async",
    "Title: lang_python_async\nContent: Python async/await: Use asyncio for IO-bound concurrency. async def + await for coroutines. asyncio.gather() for parallel IO. aiohttp for async HTTP, asyncpg for async PostgreSQL. Never mix sync blocking calls in async code.\nSolutions: Parallel IO: results = await asyncio.gather(fetch_a(), fetch_b(), fetch_c()), Async HTTP: async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: resp = await session.get(url), FastAPI: async def endpoint() is automatically async, Use asyncio.to_thread() to run sync code in async context\n"
  ],
  "1ee05c9b84b65874595d3fc06a76d9e0": [
    "lang_python_decorators",
    "Title: lang_python_decorators\nContent: Python decorators: Functions that wrap other functions. Use @functools.wraps to preserve metadata. Common patterns: @lru_cache for memoization, @property for computed attrs, @staticmethod/@classmethod for alternate constructors.\nSolutions: Decorator: def timer(func): @wraps(func) def wrapper(*args, **kw): start=time.time(); r=func(*args,**kw); print(time.time()-start); return r; return wrapper, @lru_cache(maxsize=128) for expensive pure functions, @dataclass(frozen=True) for immutable data classes, Decorator with args: def retry(times): def decorator(func): ... return decorator\n"
  ],
  "9a8613409c3ae694628a21f850a9742b": [
    "lang_python_comprehensions",
    "Title: lang_python_comprehensions\nContent: Python comprehensions: List [x for x in items if cond], Dict {k:v for k,v in pairs}, Set {x for x in items}, Generator (x for x in items). Use comprehensions over map/filter. Keep them single-line readable — use regular loops for complex logic.\nSolutions: List: names = [u.name for u in users if u.active], Dict: scores = {s.name: s.grade for s in students}, Nested: flat = [x for row in matrix for x in row], Generator for large data: sum(x*x for x in range(1000000))\n"
  ],
  "5e24aac3f8aaaeef59672351f5f591a7": [
    "lang_python_dataclasses",
    "Title: lang_python_dataclasses\nContent: Python dataclasses: @dataclass auto-generates __init__, __repr__, __eq__. Use frozen=True for immutable. field(default_factory=list) for mutable defaults. Use __post_init__ for validation. For API models, prefer Pydantic over dataclass.\nSolutions: Basic: @dataclass class User: name: str; age: int; active: bool = True, Immutable: @dataclass(frozen=True) class Point: x: float; y: float, Validation: def __post_init__(self): if self.age < 0: raise ValueError, Mutable default: items: list = field(default_factory=list)\n"
  ],
  "64407823a483550b03bb0a5b743bd5d5": [
    "lang_js_async_patterns",
    "Title: lang_js_async_patterns\nContent: JS async patterns: async/await over .then() chains. Promise.all() for parallel, Promise.allSettled() when some can fail, Promise.race() for timeouts. Use AbortController for cancellation. Handle errors with try/catch, not .catch().\nSolutions: Parallel: const [users, posts] = await Promise.all([fetchUsers(), fetchPosts()]), Timeout: const result = await Promise.race([fetch(url), timeout(5000)]), Cancel: const ctrl = new AbortController(); fetch(url, {signal: ctrl.signal}), Error: try { await api() } catch(e) { if(e.name === 'AbortError') return; throw e; }\n"
  ],
  "d29faea218c292b2488ee7baa3b480d3": [
    "lang_ts_utility_types",
    "Title: lang_ts_utility_types\nContent: TypeScript utility types: Partial<T> all optional, Required<T> all required, Pick<T,'a'|'b'> subset, Omit<T,'c'> exclude, Record<K,V> map type, Readonly<T>, Extract/Exclude for union filtering. Use these over manual type definitions.\nSolutions: Update DTO: type UserUpdate = Partial<User>, Create DTO: type UserCreate = Omit<User, 'id' | 'createdAt'>, API response: type ApiResponse<T> = { data: T; error?: string; status: number }, Discriminated union: type Result<T> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: Error }\n"
  ],
  "e4cf9d53dc851fe2bcdaa2f57e753f59": [
    "lang_ts_strict_mode",
    "Title: lang_ts_strict_mode\nContent: TypeScript strict mode: Enable strict: true in tsconfig.json. Includes strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny, strictFunctionTypes. Use 'unknown' over 'any'. Narrow types with type guards: typeof, instanceof, 'prop' in obj, discriminated unions.\nSolutions: tsconfig: {compilerOptions: {strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true}}, Type guard: function isUser(x: unknown): x is User { return typeof x === 'object' && x !== null && 'name' in x }, Narrow: if (result.kind === 'success') result.data // TypeScript knows it's SuccessResult, Use satisfies operator: const config = {...} satisfies Config\n"
  ],
  "b45aeadbf5b3538fa64f29fd8789f165": [
    "lang_js_es_modules",
    "Title: lang_js_es_modules\nContent: ES Modules: Use import/export over require/module.exports. Named exports for utilities: export function helper(). Default export for main class/component. Dynamic import() for code splitting. In Node.js: set type:module in package.json or use .mjs.\nSolutions: Named: export const API_URL = '...'; import { API_URL } from './config', Default: export default class UserService {}; import UserService from './UserService', Dynamic: const module = await import('./heavy-module'), Re-export: export { default as Button } from './Button'\n"
  ],
  "bbbb1f7d178f94bb05eb7557491e5f2f": [
    "lang_dart_null_safety",
    "Title: lang_dart_null_safety\nContent: Dart null safety: All types non-nullable by default. Use String? for nullable. Late keyword for deferred initialization. Use ?. for null-aware access, ?? for defaults, ! for force-unwrap (only when certain). Required keyword for named parameters.\nSolutions: Nullable: String? name; // can be null, Null-aware: final len = name?.length ?? 0;, Late: late final String token; // initialized before use, Required: void greet({required String name}) {}, Null check pattern: if (name case String n) print(n.length);\n"
  ],
  "456c1457b98a2c95f33d8d2673a4afa4": [
    "lang_dart_freezed",
    "Title: lang_dart_freezed\nContent: Dart Freezed package: Code generation for immutable models with copyWith, ==, hashCode, toString, JSON serialization. Use @freezed for immutable, @unfreezed for mutable. Combines with json_serializable for fromJson/toJson.\nSolutions: Model: @freezed class User with _$User { factory User({required String name, required int age}) = _User; factory User.fromJson(Map<String,dynamic> json) => _$UserFromJson(json); }, CopyWith: final updated = user.copyWith(name: 'New Name');, Union types: @freezed class AuthState { factory AuthState.authenticated(User user) = Authenticated; factory AuthState.unauthenticated() = Unauthenticated; }, Run: dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs\n"
  ],
  "7f4406e880fd837ec8c0798b0610cdcc": [
    "lang_dart_riverpod",
    "Title: lang_dart_riverpod\nContent: Riverpod state management: Compile-safe, testable, no BuildContext needed. Use @riverpod annotation for code generation. Provider types: Provider (sync), FutureProvider (async), NotifierProvider (stateful), StreamProvider (streams).\nSolutions: Simple: @riverpod String greeting(GreetingRef ref) => 'Hello';, Async: @riverpod Future<List<User>> users(UsersRef ref) async => fetchUsers();, Stateful: @riverpod class Counter extends _$Counter { @override int build() => 0; void increment() => state++; }, Consume: ref.watch(usersProvider).when(data: (d)=>..., error: (e,s)=>..., loading: ()=>...)\n"
  ],
  "29ff01587f3dd7cea08c21156c8e7bee": [
    "lang_kotlin_coroutines",
    "Title: lang_kotlin_coroutines\nContent: Kotlin Coroutines: Lightweight concurrency. launch{} for fire-and-forget, async{}/await() for results, withContext(Dispatchers.IO) for IO work. Use structured concurrency with coroutineScope{}. Flow for reactive streams.\nSolutions: Launch: viewModelScope.launch { val user = repository.getUser() }, Async: val (a, b) = coroutineScope { async{getA()} to async{getB()} }; a.await() + b.await(), IO: withContext(Dispatchers.IO) { database.query() }, Flow: fun getUsers(): Flow<List<User>> = flow { emit(api.fetchUsers()) }\n"
  ],
  "1903f7b9d6156e8ddeee051ea314d794": [
    "lang_kotlin_data_class",
    "Title: lang_kotlin_data_class\nContent: Kotlin data classes: Concise immutable models with auto-generated equals(), hashCode(), toString(), copy(), componentN(). Use sealed classes for state machines. Use value classes for type-safe wrappers with zero overhead.\nSolutions: Data: data class User(val name: String, val age: Int), Copy: val updated = user.copy(name = \"New\"), Sealed: sealed class Result<T> { data class Success<T>(val data: T): Result<T>(); data class Failure(val error: Throwable): Result<Nothing>() }, Value: @JvmInline value class Email(val value: String)\n"
  ],
  "7a692d828299e6fbfd14ad9c3e827f6c": [
    "lang_go_error_handling",
    "Title: lang_go_error_handling\nContent: Go error handling: Return (result, error) tuples. Check errors immediately. Use fmt.Errorf with %w for wrapping. errors.Is/As for comparison. Custom error types with Error() string method. Never ignore errors with _.\nSolutions: Return: func getUser(id int) (*User, error) { if id <= 0 { return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid id: %d\", id) } }, Wrap: return fmt.Errorf(\"getUser: %w\", err), Check: if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) { return nil, ErrNotFound }, Custom: type NotFoundError struct { ID int }; func (e NotFoundError) Error() string { return fmt.Sprintf(\"not found: %d\", e.ID) }\n"
  ],
  "411578fb89a1fd94fcffba2f97051a51": [
    "lang_go_concurrency",
    "Title: lang_go_concurrency\nContent: Go concurrency: goroutines + channels. Use 'go func(){}()' for goroutines. Channels for communication: ch := make(chan Type). Select for multiplexing. sync.WaitGroup for coordination. context.Context for cancellation and timeouts.\nSolutions: Worker pool: for i := 0; i < workers; i++ { go func() { for job := range jobs { results <- process(job) } }() }, Select: select { case msg := <-ch: handle(msg); case <-ctx.Done(): return ctx.Err() }, WaitGroup: var wg sync.WaitGroup; wg.Add(n); go func(){defer wg.Done(); work()}(); wg.Wait(), Timeout: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second); defer cancel()\n"
  ],
  "235ba7f06eb856edea5df47d3af76e52": [
    "lang_rust_ownership",
    "Title: lang_rust_ownership\nContent: Rust ownership: Each value has one owner. Ownership moves on assignment. Borrow with & (immutable) or &mut (mutable, exclusive). Lifetimes ensure references are valid. Clone for explicit copies. Use Arc<Mutex<T>> for shared mutable state across threads.\nSolutions: Move: let s2 = s1; // s1 is now invalid, Borrow: fn len(s: &str) -> usize { s.len() } // immutable borrow, Mut borrow: fn push(v: &mut Vec<i32>) { v.push(42); } // exclusive access, Clone: let s2 = s1.clone(); // both valid, explicit copy\n"
  ],
  "28af7b13ecc22d00ae1ec268775feb2b": [
    "lang_csharp_linq",
    "Title: lang_csharp_linq\nContent: C# LINQ: Query collections fluently. Method syntax preferred: .Where(x => x.Active).Select(x => x.Name).ToList(). Deferred execution — enumerate once. Use async variants: await collection.ToListAsync(). IQueryable for database, IEnumerable for memory.\nSolutions: Filter+Map: var names = users.Where(u => u.Age > 18).Select(u => u.Name).ToList();, Group: var byDept = employees.GroupBy(e => e.Dept).Select(g => new { Dept = g.Key, Count = g.Count() });, Join: var result = orders.Join(products, o => o.ProductId, p => p.Id, (o, p) => new { o.Qty, p.Name });, Async (EF): var users = await db.Users.Where(u => u.Active).ToListAsync();\n"
  ],
  "c87a514163068c18d9b4bef85f2ad848": [
    "lang_csharp_records",
    "Title: lang_csharp_records\nContent: C# records: Immutable reference types with value equality. record class for reference, record struct for value type. With-expressions for copies. Perfect for DTOs, events, domain objects.\nSolutions: Simple: public record User(string Name, int Age);, With: var updated = user with { Name = \"New\" };, Record struct: public readonly record struct Point(double X, double Y);, Deconstruct: var (name, age) = user;\n"
  ],
  "e737fd0173997f76c834924210b6d08b": [
    "lang_bash_best_practices",
    "Title: lang_bash_best_practices\nContent: Bash best practices: Always set -euo pipefail at top. Quote all variables \"$var\". Use [[ ]] over [ ]. Use local variables in functions. Shellcheck for linting. Prefer printf over echo for portability.\nSolutions: Header: #!/usr/bin/env bash; set -euo pipefail, Variables: readonly CONFIG_DIR=\"${HOME}/.config\"; local result=\"$(command)\", Conditionals: [[ -f \"$file\" ]] && [[ \"$str\" == \"expected\" ]], Arrays: declare -a items=(\"one\" \"two\"); for item in \"${items[@]}\"; do echo \"$item\"; done\n"
  ],
  "170fedeac11a4b09afb9bec61e231c23": [
    "lang_sql_optimization",
    "Title: lang_sql_optimization\nContent: SQL optimization: Use indexes on WHERE/JOIN/ORDER BY columns. EXPLAIN ANALYZE to check query plans. Avoid SELECT * — list needed columns. Use CTEs for readability. Batch inserts (VALUES lists). Parameterized queries always.\nSolutions: Index: CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);, CTE: WITH active AS (SELECT * FROM users WHERE active) SELECT * FROM active JOIN orders ..., Batch: INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('a','a@x'), ('b','b@x'), ('c','c@x');, NEVER: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '\" + input + \"' -- SQL injection!\n"
  ],
  "e5650ce42e3cce9ba12bbad5e3e32642": [
    "lang_sql_window_functions",
    "Title: lang_sql_window_functions\nContent: SQL window functions: ROW_NUMBER(), RANK(), DENSE_RANK() for numbering. LAG/LEAD for prev/next row. SUM/AVG OVER(PARTITION BY) for running totals. NTILE for bucketing. PARTITION BY groups, ORDER BY sorts within each group.\nSolutions: Row number: SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY dept ORDER BY salary DESC) rn FROM employees, Running total: SELECT date, amount, SUM(amount) OVER(ORDER BY date) running_total FROM sales, Prev value: SELECT date, value, LAG(value) OVER(ORDER BY date) prev_value FROM metrics, Top N per group: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY category ORDER BY score DESC) rn FROM items) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3\n"
  ],
  "206b925cd9df7efecb9318a81121800f": [
    "Java Builder Pattern",
    "Title: Java Builder Pattern\nDescription: Builder pattern for complex object construction with fluent API\nWhen to use: When constructors have 4+ parameters or optional fields\nCode Example:\npublic class User { private String name; private int age; public static class Builder { private String name; private int age; public Builder name(String n) { this.name=n; return this; } public Builder age(int a) { this.age=a; return this; } public User build() { User u = new User(); u.name=this.name; u.age=this.age; return u; } } }\n"
  ],
  "83dd38b13bcf6e6916713c9981b49a08": [
    "Spring Data Repository",
    "Title: Spring Data Repository\nDescription: Repository interface with custom query methods following Spring Data naming conventions\nWhen to use: Data access layer in Spring Boot applications\nCode Example:\npublic interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, Long> { Optional<User> findByEmail(String email); List<User> findByActiveTrue(); @Query(\"SELECT u FROM User u WHERE u.createdAt > :date\") List<User> findRecentUsers(@Param(\"date\") LocalDateTime date); }\n"
  ],
  "2cf9a2b0a3bbb7d32a3e0b05d7b6ff94": [
    "Service Layer Pattern",
    "Title: Service Layer Pattern\nDescription: Transactional service with dependency injection, validation, and error handling\nWhen to use: Business logic layer in Spring Boot\nCode Example:\n@Service @RequiredArgsConstructor public class UserService { private final UserRepository repo; private final PasswordEncoder encoder; @Transactional public User create(CreateUserDTO dto) { if(repo.existsByEmail(dto.email())) throw new DuplicateException(); User u = new User(); u.setEmail(dto.email()); u.setPassword(encoder.encode(dto.password())); return repo.save(u); } }\n"
  ],
  "0f01e8d5c4ad842c588b493ec275ed3e": [
    "FastAPI CRUD Router",
    "Title: FastAPI CRUD Router\nDescription: Complete CRUD router with Pydantic models, dependency injection, and error handling\nWhen to use: REST API endpoints in FastAPI\nCode Example:\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix='/users', tags=['users'])\n@router.get('/', response_model=list[UserOut])\nasync def list_users(db: Session = Depends(get_db), skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):\n    return db.query(User).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()\n@router.post('/', response_model=UserOut, status_code=201)\nasync def create_user(user: UserCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    db_user = User(**user.dict()); db.add(db_user); db.commit(); return db_user\n"
  ],
  "51f3a022595cda5b2d052ac06a7724ed": [
    "Context Manager Pattern",
    "Title: Context Manager Pattern\nDescription: Custom context manager for resource management using __enter__/__exit__ or @contextmanager\nWhen to use: Database connections, file handling, temporary state\nCode Example:\n@contextmanager\ndef managed_connection(url):\n    conn = create_connection(url)\n    try:\n        yield conn\n    finally:\n        conn.close()\n# Usage: with managed_connection(url) as conn: conn.execute(query)\n"
  ],
  "08eb6e38e70464be2348bc4e040538b6": [
    "TypeScript API Client",
    "Title: TypeScript API Client\nDescription: Type-safe API client with interceptors, error handling, and generic request methods\nWhen to use: Frontend or backend HTTP client\nCode Example:\nclass ApiClient { constructor(private baseUrl: string, private token?: string) {} private async request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> { const res = await fetch(`${this.baseUrl}${path}`, { method, headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json', ...(this.token && {'Authorization':`Bearer ${this.token}`})}, body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined }); if(!res.ok) throw new ApiError(res.status, await res.text()); return res.json(); } get<T>(path: string) { return this.request<T>('GET', path); } post<T>(path: string, body: unknown) { return this.request<T>('POST', path, body); } }\n"
  ],
  "894088e1ad4e7cbf3467f94d339d35a4": [
    "React Custom Hook",
    "Title: React Custom Hook\nDescription: Custom React hook with loading/error states and TypeScript generics\nWhen to use: Reusable data fetching or state logic in React\nCode Example:\nfunction useAsync<T>(asyncFn: () => Promise<T>, deps: unknown[] = []) { const [state, setState] = useState<{data?:T; error?:Error; loading:boolean}>({loading:true}); useEffect(() => { let cancelled = false; setState({loading:true}); asyncFn().then(data => { if(!cancelled) setState({data, loading:false}); }).catch(error => { if(!cancelled) setState({error, loading:false}); }); return () => { cancelled = true; }; }, deps); return state; }\n"
  ],
  "6a0c94f594c47919548986bd7424c85d": [
    "Flutter Repository Pattern",
    "Title: Flutter Repository Pattern\nDescription: Repository abstraction over data sources (API + local cache) with error handling\nWhen to use: Data layer in Flutter apps\nCode Example:\nabstract class UserRepository { Future<List<User>> getUsers(); Future<User> getUserById(String id); }\nclass UserRepositoryImpl implements UserRepository { final ApiClient api; final LocalCache cache; UserRepositoryImpl(this.api, this.cache);\n  @override Future<List<User>> getUsers() async { try { final users = await api.fetchUsers(); await cache.saveUsers(users); return users; } catch (_) { return cache.getUsers(); } } }\n"
  ],
  "16920fbee02357093a7de9bf1f688acc": [
    "Flutter BLoC Pattern",
    "Title: Flutter BLoC Pattern\nDescription: Business Logic Component with events, states, and stream transformations\nWhen to use: Complex state management in Flutter\nCode Example:\n// Events\nsealed class AuthEvent {}\nclass LoginRequested extends AuthEvent { final String email, password; LoginRequested(this.email, this.password); }\n// States\nsealed class AuthState {}\nclass AuthInitial extends AuthState {}\nclass AuthLoading extends AuthState {}\nclass AuthSuccess extends AuthState { final User user; AuthSuccess(this.user); }\nclass AuthFailure extends AuthState { final String message; AuthFailure(this.message); }\n// BLoC\nclass AuthBloc extends Bloc<AuthEvent, AuthState> { AuthBloc(this.repo) : super(AuthInitial()) { on<LoginRequested>(_onLogin); } }\n"
  ],
  "de54aa6e02135d084ec4ebdf9f9ab68d": [
    "Go HTTP Handler",
    "Title: Go HTTP Handler\nDescription: HTTP handler with middleware, JSON encoding, and proper error responses\nWhen to use: REST API endpoints in Go\nCode Example:\nfunc handleGetUser(svc UserService) http.HandlerFunc { return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { id := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\"); user, err := svc.GetByID(r.Context(), id); if err != nil { if errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound) { http.Error(w, \"not found\", 404); return }; http.Error(w, \"internal error\", 500); return }; w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\",\"application/json\"); json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(user) } }\n"
  ],
  "ef15712faa3618be6fd97b256d8e76b9": [
    "Android ViewModel",
    "Title: Android ViewModel\nDescription: ViewModel with StateFlow, coroutines, and repository pattern\nWhen to use: UI state management in Android\nCode Example:\nclass UserViewModel(private val repo: UserRepository) : ViewModel() { private val _state = MutableStateFlow<UiState<List<User>>>(UiState.Loading); val state: StateFlow<UiState<List<User>>> = _state.asStateFlow(); init { loadUsers() }; private fun loadUsers() { viewModelScope.launch { _state.value = UiState.Loading; try { _state.value = UiState.Success(repo.getUsers()) } catch(e: Exception) { _state.value = UiState.Error(e.message ?: \"Unknown\") } } } }\n"
  ],
  "1270527da92b36b9e710d7051e62018d": [
    "Accessibility Checklist",
    "Title: Accessibility Checklist\nDescription: Ensure all web applications meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards\nDO: Use semantic HTML elements (button, nav, main, article, aside), Add alt text to all informative images, Ensure 4.5:1 color contrast ratio for normal text, Support full keyboard navigation with visible focus indicators, Use aria-live regions for dynamic content updates, Test with screen reader (NVDA/VoiceOver) before release\nDONT: Don't use div/span as clickable elements without role and keyboard handler, Don't rely solely on color to convey information, Don't remove focus outlines without providing alternative, Don't use placeholder text as the only label\n"
  ],
  "c01237bc75b5ea09253630b92fe0d098": [
    "Documentation Standards",
    "Title: Documentation Standards\nDescription: Maintain clear, up-to-date documentation for all projects\nDO: Write README with setup, run, test, deploy instructions, Document API endpoints with OpenAPI/Swagger, Create ADRs for significant technical decisions, Maintain CHANGELOG.md with each release, Include inline code comments for complex business logic only, Write runbooks for production incident response\nDONT: Don't write comments that restate the code, Don't let docs become stale — update docs with code changes, Don't document internal implementation details in public API docs, Don't skip error code documentation\n"
  ],
  "8e4622873150ecafc5bd5f7289a984e8": [
    "Code Review Etiquette",
    "Title: Code Review Etiquette\nDescription: Conduct productive, respectful code reviews that improve code quality\nDO: Keep PRs under 400 lines of code, Provide constructive feedback with reasoning, Approve with minor nits — don't block on style preferences, Use automated linters for style enforcement, Review for correctness, security, and edge cases first, Respond to reviews within 24 hours\nDONT: Don't leave vague comments like 'This is wrong', Don't request changes for personal style preferences covered by linter, Don't approve without actually reading the code, Don't pile on — one reviewer flagging an issue is enough\n"
  ],
  "8f8bad45c5e1e073595ec4b402815cf5": [
    "Internationalization Guidelines",
    "Title: Internationalization Guidelines\nDescription: Build applications ready for multiple languages and locales\nDO: Externalize all user-facing strings from day one, Use ICU message format for plurals and interpolation, Format dates, numbers, currency with Intl API or locale-aware libraries, Support RTL layouts with CSS logical properties, Test with pseudo-localization to catch hardcoded strings, Allow 30-50% text expansion for translations\nDONT: Don't concatenate translated strings — use templates with placeholders, Don't hardcode date/number formats, Don't assume all languages are LTR, Don't use images containing text — use SVGs with translatable labels\n"
  ],
  "7f0308c9cc34b04fe6ff02b85fe126d7": [
    "Mobile Development Best Practices",
    "Title: Mobile Development Best Practices\nDescription: Build high-quality mobile applications across platforms\nDO: Handle offline scenarios gracefully with local caching, Optimize for battery — minimize background work, batch network requests, Request permissions at point of use, not on launch, Test on real devices, not just emulators, Support deep linking for navigation from external sources, Implement crash reporting (Crashlytics, Sentry)\nDONT: Don't block the UI thread with heavy computation, Don't ignore platform design guidelines (Material Design, HIG), Don't request unnecessary permissions, Don't hardcode API URLs — use environment configs\n"
  ],
  "8a21aee628991ad13b51da7d5fe85010": [
    "Serverless Best Practices",
    "Title: Serverless Best Practices\nDescription: Design efficient, cost-effective serverless applications\nDO: Keep functions small and single-purpose, Use environment variables for configuration, Implement structured logging with request correlation IDs, Set memory/timeout limits appropriate to function workload, Use provisioned concurrency for latency-critical functions, Design for idempotency — functions may be invoked multiple times\nDONT: Don't use serverless for long-running tasks (>15 min), Don't store state in function memory — use external store, Don't ignore cold start impact on user experience, Don't create monolithic functions that do everything\n"
  ],
  "d2804ca5b59f169fc4cf2e021bdd57ed": [
    "practice_a11y_basics",
    "Title: practice_a11y_basics\nContent: Web accessibility (WCAG 2.1): Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust. Use semantic HTML (nav, main, article, button). Add alt text to images. Ensure 4.5:1 color contrast ratio. Support keyboard navigation. Test with screen readers.\nSolutions: Semantic HTML: <button> not <div onclick>, <nav> not <div class='nav'>, Alt text: <img alt='Dashboard showing user growth chart' />; alt='' for decorative images, Contrast: Text 4.5:1 ratio (AA), large text 3:1. Use WebAIM contrast checker, Focus: Visible focus indicators, logical tab order, skip-to-content link\n"
  ],
  "67276fe483ee4acd1622365b3fa3c7e7": [
    "practice_a11y_aria",
    "Title: practice_a11y_aria\nContent: ARIA attributes: Use only when semantic HTML is insufficient. aria-label for custom buttons, aria-live for dynamic content, role for custom widgets. First rule: don't use ARIA if native HTML element exists. Test with NVDA, VoiceOver, or axe-core.\nSolutions: aria-label: <button aria-label='Close dialog'>X</button>, aria-live: <div aria-live='polite'>3 new notifications</div> — announced by screen reader, role: <div role='tablist'><button role='tab' aria-selected='true'>Tab 1</button></div>, Testing: npm install @axe-core/react; axe-core browser extension; Lighthouse audit\n"
  ],
  "19e9421e40db089bc605196be36646c6": [
    "practice_i18n",
    "Title: practice_i18n\nContent: Internationalization (i18n): Externalize all user-facing strings. Use ICU message format for plurals and interpolation. Format dates/numbers with Intl API. Support RTL layouts with logical CSS properties (margin-inline-start).\nSolutions: React: import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next'; const { t } = useTranslation(); t('welcome', { name }), Plurals: '{count, plural, =0 {No items} one {1 item} other {# items}}', Dates: new Intl.DateTimeFormat('de-DE', { dateStyle: 'long' }).format(date), RTL: Use margin-inline-start/end instead of margin-left/right\n"
  ],
  "0647039720c20136fe8c4365f878db5a": [
    "practice_i18n_flutter",
    "Title: practice_i18n_flutter\nContent: Flutter i18n: Use flutter_localizations + intl package. Define .arb files per locale. Generate with gen-l10n. Access via AppLocalizations.of(context).welcome. Support 50+ locales with minimal code changes.\nSolutions: Setup: flutter pub add flutter_localizations intl, Define: lib/l10n/app_en.arb → { 'welcome': 'Welcome {name}', '@welcome': { 'placeholders': { 'name': {} } } }, Generate: flutter gen-l10n → generates AppLocalizations class, Use: Text(AppLocalizations.of(context)!.welcome('John'))\n"
  ],
  "4f2a55faa58747c1489d935f1951e206": [
    "practice_api_docs",
    "Title: practice_api_docs\nContent: API documentation: Use OpenAPI 3.0 (Swagger) spec. Auto-generate from annotations. Include request/response examples, error codes, authentication. Spring: springdoc-openapi. FastAPI: auto-generates /docs. Keep docs in sync with code.\nSolutions: Spring: @Operation(summary='Create user') @ApiResponse(responseCode='201'), FastAPI: OpenAPI auto-generated at /docs (Swagger UI) and /redoc, Best: Include curl examples, error response examples, rate limit info, Versioning: OpenAPI spec per API version, publish to developer portal\n"
  ],
  "d85d0f93fa4b6f21dfd7925fc945fe92": [
    "practice_adr",
    "Title: practice_adr\nContent: Architecture Decision Records (ADR): Document key technical decisions. Format: Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences. Store in docs/adr/ in repo. Number sequentially (0001, 0002). Immutable once accepted — supersede with new ADR.\nSolutions: Template: # ADR-0001: Use PostgreSQL for primary database\n## Status: Accepted\n## Context: Need ACID transactions...\n## Decision: PostgreSQL 15\n## Consequences: Team needs SQL skills, managed hosting cost, When: Language/framework choice, architecture change, security-critical decisions, Tools: adr-tools CLI for scaffolding, or just markdown files, Rule: Never delete/edit accepted ADRs — create new one that supersedes\n"
  ],
  "e61652aa5ac64e7e4b3014eed8104708": [
    "practice_changelog",
    "Title: practice_changelog\nContent: Changelog management: Follow Keep a Changelog format. Sections: Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, Security. Link each version to git diff. Automate with conventional commits.
